notes.introduction: An exposition of Joshua 2:8-21 on Rahab's declaration of God's supremacy to the two Israelite spies, set within the broader narrative of Israel's preparation to enter Canaan. Part of a series on declaring the greatness of God, the message traces what Rahab heard, what she concluded, what she proclaimed, and what she requested, with extended comparison to Old Testament typologies of salvation and refuge.

sections.heading: What Rahab Revealed: Heard and Knew
What Rahab Declared: God of Heaven and Earth
What Rahab Requested: Plea for Protection
The Scarlet Thread and One Place of Safety
Rahab's Immediate Faith and Its Evidence
Joshua 6: Rahab and Her Household Delivered
